signals.trend: World Cup micro-merch: licensed collectibles lane on TikTok Shop
signals.why: Event commerce is consolidating into a few 'officially licensed' giftable sellers — MINISO's FIFA-licensed pins and magnets put it in the winning lane, validating its IP-collectibles pivot into the knockout rounds.
signals.evidence: MINISO's trophy bottle-opener fridge magnet at $18.99 shows the theme scaled down to an impulse collectible; official-gift winners are concentrated in a handful of sellers.

sources.name: FastMoss
sources.title: What World Cup Fans Are Actually Buying on TikTok Shop

signals.trend: Meme-flow gauge: Wendy's rally extends into the holiday week
signals.why: Reddit-driven retail flows kept a sub-$3B restaurant name up ~50% off lows into July — sentiment-led and fundamentals-light, but a live gauge of small-cap consumer risk appetite.
signals.evidence: Shares closed 26% higher on June 24 — the biggest jump since June 2021 — after earlier surging as much as 42% (Bloomberg); July 2 coverage warned against chasing.

sources.name: Bloomberg
sources.title: Wendy's Stock Soars 26% as Meme Traders Flock to Burger Chain

sources.name: The Motley Fool
sources.title: Don't Chase Wendy's Meme Stock Rally

watchlist.trend: Prediction markets are the World Cup's breakout consumer product
watchlist.why: World Cup event contracts turned prediction markets into mainstream consumer apps — record volumes and 78.5% of June betting-app installs, a structural share shift away from listed sportsbooks (DKNG, FLUT).
watchlist.evidence: Kalshi kept daily volume above $1 billion since the tournament kicked off June 11, closing June with more than $31 billion in trading volume, while Polymarket added $10.8 billion — a record.

sources.name: CNBC
sources.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup boosts prediction market volumes

sources.name: DeFi Rate
sources.title: Kalshi, Polymarket Keep Gaining DAUs During World Cup as Sportsbooks Peak Early
